note_data.title: Nutrition in humans
keyPoints: **Balanced diet** contains all nutrients in correct proportions to maintain health
**Carbohydrates** provide energy for respiration and should form the largest proportion of the diet
**Proteins** are needed for growth and repair of tissues and making enzymes
**Lipids** provide energy storage, insulation and protection of organs
**Vitamins** are needed in small amounts to prevent deficiency diseases - vitamin C prevents scurvy, vitamin D prevents rickets
**Minerals** like iron is needed for making haemoglobin and calcium for strong bones and teeth
**Fibre** prevents constipation and reduces risk of colon cancer
**Mechanical digestion** breaks food into smaller pieces to increase surface area for enzymes
**Chemical digestion** uses enzymes to break down large molecules into smaller soluble molecules for absorption
**Absorption** occurs mainly in the small intestine where nutrients pass into the blood

definitions.definition: The process by which organisms obtain and use food for growth, repair, energy and maintaining health
The breakdown of large insoluble food molecules into small soluble molecules that can be absorbed into the blood
A diet containing all nutrients in the correct proportions to maintain health
A condition caused by eating too much, too little or wrong types of nutrients
Wave-like contractions of smooth muscle that push food along the alimentary canal

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give a detailed account of the process step by step
Give similarities and differences between two things

modelAnswer.question: Explain why protein is an important component of a balanced diet. [3 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Protein is needed for growth of new tissues in children and adolescents. Protein is required for repair of damaged tissues after injury or exercise. Protein is used to make enzymes which catalyse metabolic reactions in the body.

commonMistakes: Never say 'protein gives energy' as main function - always emphasize growth and repair first
Students confuse absorption and digestion - absorption is uptake into blood, digestion is breakdown
Don't say 'vitamins give energy' - they are needed in small amounts for specific functions only
Never write 'food goes to stomach' - always use 'food passes into stomach'
Students forget that mechanical digestion increases surface area for chemical digestion
examinerTips: This topic appears in every exam paper - learn the functions of all nutrients
Always distinguish between mechanical and chemical digestion in your answers
Draw and label the digestive system diagram - commonly worth 4-6 marks
Learn specific deficiency diseases for vitamins and minerals
Remember that enzymes work best at body temperature and neutral pH
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Digestive system showing mouth, oesophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine, liver, pancreas and their functions in digestion and absorption
